title: What Your Doctor Won't Tell You About Fasting Insulin

chunks.title: Importance of Metabolic Health
chunks.summary: The discussion starts with the assertion that metabolic health should be the core focus in Western medicine when tackling chronic diseases. The speakers emphasize that many physicians do not test patients’ metabolic health, which would significantly impact patient outcomes.
chunks.transcript: I want to talk to Bobby about this I want to talk to RFK Jr about this when we're in DC I love that you said metabolic Health needs to be the tip of the spear that should be the central point of everything we do in Western medicine around chronic disease because as Kelly and Casey have so eloquently said and brought to the Forefront again like that is really Ground Zero that is step one like if you just focus on a patient's metabolic Health you're going to get everything else is gonna fall into the line but most doctors won't even do a single test that looks at metabolic health so I tweeted and I would love for the federal government to subsidize fasting insulin talk about because I you told me this yeah I I I

chunks.title: Challenges with Insurance and Testing
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the obstacles posed by insurance companies that deter doctors from ordering necessary blood tests for metabolic health. They highlight the issues around reimbursement and how it limits providers' ability to offer proactive care.
chunks.transcript: heard you on Rogan talking about this and it's something that's interesting as a doctor if I order blood tests and the insurance company doesn't War me to order those blood test they can come back to me and say hey why are you ordering a fasting insulin we're not going to pay for that and in fact we're not going to cover we're not going to reimburse you for seeing these patients like as we talked about earlier in the podcast these insurance companies are really dictating they are they are putting they are tying our reimburse you yeah and unless you're on a cash pay model as a physician that's death I'm actually very glad you brought that up because before I started ways dwell in my Pharmacy revive I owned blood labs and I would go out and I would educate Primary Care clinicians throughout the country on the importance of getting proactive and predictive and I would explain to them you can't you can't treat treat what you can't see right and we can't cure what we don't know is there and the only way to uncover what's really happening with the patient is to do the Deep dive and I would educate them and these clinicians would go this makes total sense I want to implement these comprehensive blood panels into my practice and then within a month they'll get a letter from the insurance company why are you pulling comprehensive blood work we don't think you have clinical necessity we don't think you should be doing this and they'll send them warning threatening to kick them off of that contract and so the doctors would call me and say hey I'm sorry man I'm done I'm not pulling blood work anymore I got a threatening letter from Blue Cross or I got a threatening letter from Etna oh

chunks.title: Impact of Opioid Crisis on Health Care
chunks.summary: The speakers address the opioid crisis and how insurance companies have contributed to the problem by promoting opioids over non-addictive alternatives. This segment emphasizes the need for pharmacogenetic testing to better manage pain medication prescriptions.

chunks.title: Corruption in the Health Care System
chunks.summary: There’s a critical examination of systemic corruption within healthcare, pointing fingers at various entities from pharmaceutical companies to regulatory boards. The speakers argue that profit motives have consistently overridden true patient care.

chunks.title: Need for Improved Testing Practices
chunks.summary: The discussion highlights the necessity for affordable testing, such as fasting insulin tests, to better identify metabolic dysfunction in populations. There’s a call for governmental support to enhance the accessibility of these critical health metrics.
chunks.transcript: much stock in Black Rock St Street in Vanguard is a Sackler family own bro I'm they're not the only ones but who the [ __ ] knows but yeah okay so I just want to make sure we I emphasize this point that's that's why wild I feel like a way to fix this would be like you know fasting insulin is 30 bucks you know what if the federal government subsidized that the insurance company can't tell you no a single blood test would zero in it's not perfect but it's damn sensitive and specific and we would be able to tell overnight that we have this huge epidemic of metabolic dysfunction in this country the reference range for fasting insulin is like it goes up to 22 or 26 micro IU per ml that's wild dude everyone you and I

chunks.title: Personal Health Stories and Insights
chunks.summary: In a more personal narrative, the speakers share their own health journeys, including struggles with obesity and hormonal imbalances. They discuss how comprehensive blood tests revealed underlying health issues that dietary changes alone couldn't address.
